title: Introduction to Global Business
catalogDescription: Prepares students to understand the most important aspects of the international political economy. Emphasis on the important relationships among nations and business and economic institutions that influence students' performances as managers, consumers, and citizens. Prerequisite: ECON 200.

title: Global Business Strategy in Emerging Markets
catalogDescription: Explores challenges and opportunities involved in doing business in emerging and developing markets. Topics include: analysis of foreign economic, cultural and political environments; market entry decisions; competitive strategies; management of risks; roles of technology, trade policy, macroeconomic and exchange rates. Prerequisite: I BUS 300 or JSIS 222; may not be repeated.
